key_insight: MTP speedups in RL are limited by entropy growth, and Bebop recovers high acceptance by pairing rejection sampling with an end-to-end TV loss.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical way to cut rollout latency and improve end-to-end RL training throughput for large language models.
method: Analyze how RL-induced entropy changes constrain MTP acceptance rates.
Use probabilistic rejection sampling instead of greedy draft sampling to reduce entropy disturbance.
Train with an end-to-end TV loss to directly optimize multi-step acceptance rather than cross-entropy or KL.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports about 10% higher acceptance rates, up to 95% acceptance, up to 25% extra inference throughput, and up to 1.8x end-to-end RL acceleration on Qwen3.5/3.6/3.7 across math, code, and agentic tasks.
watch_item: Gains depend on stable integration of rejection sampling and MTP into the RL pipeline, so implementation overhead and task-specific tuning may affect real-world speedups.
paper_title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
